methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Tritone Technologies is a Series B-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ofer Ben-Zur. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of four same-sector, same- or adjacent-stage comparables: Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B Series B), Hadrian ($1.6B Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B Series C). This reflects a broader ecosystem of nine private robotics firms and one public comparable.

The peer-median method is the sole applicable triangulation approach given available data. The four-company median valuation is $1.6B, with the peer set ranging from $1.0B to $1.8B. This method provides the primary analytical anchor for valuation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $1.9B (high), centered on $1.6B. Key uncertainties include the limited methodological depth—only peer-median triangulation is available—and stage dispersion within the peer set, where two comparables are at Series C rather than Series B. The reliance on a four-company sample also constrains confidence. Refinement would benefit from revenue multiples, growth rate normalization, or comparable transaction data to triangulate against the peer median.
